hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2 (res 192/193/195/197) and TM5 (205/208/209/212) regions, while mutating position 6 to acidic residues (E/D) will introduce repulsive interactions with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5, thereby increasing selectivity margin.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis is valid but incomplete. Selectivity is not being optimized directly, and structural failures suggest that the design protocol is not generating stable, well-folded candidates. The next iteration should prioritize selectivity-driven design and structural robustness.

proposed_changes.rationale: Prioritizing selectivity (Δmargin) over raw ddG will ensure that candidates are not only strong binders but also selective for SSTR2 over off-targets like SSTR3/SSTR5.

These positions are known to contribute to conserved pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target binding without affecting the pharmacophore core (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
